muxt generate --use-receiver-type=Server
muxt check

exec go test -race -count=1

-- template.gohtml --
{{- define "GET /endpoint Endpoint(execute)" -}}[{{- .Result -}}]{{- end -}}
-- go.mod --
module server

go 1.22
-- server.go --
package server

import (
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templatesFS embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templatesFS, "*"))

type Server struct{}

// secondCallErr records the error returned by the second callback invocation so
// the test can assert the callback rejects being called more than once.
var secondCallErr error

// Endpoint invokes the render callback twice. The callback renders at most once:
// the first call renders, the second returns an error (ExecuteTemplate must not
// re-mutate the shared template data).
func (Server) Endpoint(execute func(string) error) error {
	if err := execute("first"); err != nil {
		return err
	}
	secondCallErr = execute("second")
	return nil
}
-- server_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"testing"
)

func TestEndpoint(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	TemplateRoutes(mux, Server{})

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/endpoint", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if got, want := rec.Body.String(), "[first]"; got != want {
		t.Fatalf("body = %q, want %q (callback must render at most once)", got, want)
	}
	if secondCallErr == nil {
		t.Fatal("second execute call should return an error")
	}
}
